preamble: The transformation from a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive (bʱ) to a voiceless aspirated bilabial plosive (pʰ) represents a significant historical sound change, primarily characterized by devoicing while retaining aspiration. This shift is notably observed in the evolution of Proto-Indo-European into various daughter languages, particularly Ancient Greek, where the Proto-Indo-European voiced aspirates became voiceless aspirates.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Aspiration retention, Loss of breathy voice (murmur) if bʱ was phonetically breathy-voiced

examples.note: The Proto-Indo-European voiced aspirated bilabial stop *bʰ became the voiceless aspirated bilabial stop pʰ in Ancient Greek.
